reasoning: Score is a weighted blend of liquidity, credit, AI fundamentals, breadth, valuation, macro, and geopolitical proxies.
Each subscore uses stable, explainable market-derived indicators so records remain comparable over time.
This checks whether the actual AI stock group is acting healthy.
If NVDA, SMH, and SOXX are weak, the AI story may still be good but the trade is not confirming it.
This asks: are investors still comfortable taking risk?
If bond-market risk appetite is weak, be careful adding to volatile AI names.

contributions.label: VIX fear
contributions.why: VIX shows whether fear is spreading into broad market pricing.

contributions.label: Oil shock
contributions.why: A fast oil move can lift inflation fear and hurt growth-stock multiples.

contributions.label: Gold bid
contributions.why: Gold rising quickly can mean investors are paying for safety.

contributions.label: Dollar bid
contributions.why: A stronger dollar often tightens global financial conditions.

contributions.label: QQQ shock
contributions.why: QQQ weakness shows the risk is hitting growth and mega-cap tech.

contributions.label: Energy over AI
contributions.why: Energy outperforming chips can signal rotation away from AI toward oil shock beneficiaries.

reasoning: This checks whether a shock is moving from headlines into oil, safe havens, volatility, and AI stocks.
If oil, gold, dollar, and VIX rise together while QQQ/SMH weaken, reduce risk before the story gets priced in.

contributions.label: VIX calm
contributions.why: Lower fear makes it easier for money to stay in AI and growth stocks.

contributions.label: Dollar move
contributions.why: A stronger dollar can pull money away from risk assets.

contributions.label: 10Y yield level
contributions.why: Lower or stable yields are friendlier to growth stocks.

contributions.label: 10Y 20-day move
contributions.why: Falling yields usually give AI stocks more breathing room.

contributions.label: SPY trend
contributions.why: S&P 500 above its 50-day average means the broad market is still holding up.

contributions.label: QQQ trend
contributions.why: QQQ below its 50-day average warns that tech/growth may be losing support.

contributions.label: HYG trend
contributions.why: High-yield bonds below trend suggest investors are less comfortable taking risk.

contributions.label: SMH trend
contributions.why: Chip-stock trend matters because AI leadership depends heavily on semiconductors.

reasoning: This asks whether today's funding environment is friendly to AI and growth stocks.
It combines fear, the dollar, rates, credit, broad-market trend, and chip-stock trend.
Rates and the dollar can push growth stocks around.
If rates rise fast, AI stocks can fall even when company news is still good.
This asks whether the rally is broad or just a few big names.
A broad rally is safer. A narrow rally means AI leaders can get crowded fast.
This checks whether prices have already run too far.
If this weakens, new buys need more patience because the easy move may already be priced in.
